title: Simple DVFS Algorithm
description: Basic algorithm for dynamic voltage and frequency scaling based on CPU utilization
code:
ALGORITHM DVFS_Algorithm
BEGIN
 REPEAT EVERY sampling_interval:
 utilization = get_cpu_utilization()
 
 IF utilization > 80% THEN
 SET voltage = HIGH_VOLTAGE
 SET frequency = HIGH_FREQUENCY
 target_state = P0
 ELSE IF utilization > 50% THEN
 SET voltage = MEDIUM_VOLTAGE
 SET frequency = MEDIUM_FREQUENCY
 target_state = P2
 ELSE IF utilization > 20% THEN
 SET voltage = LOW_VOLTAGE
 SET frequency = LOW_FREQUENCY
 target_state = P4
 ELSE
 target_state = C6_DEEP_SLEEP
 END IF
 
 APPLY power_state(target_state)
 END REPEAT
END
explanation: This simple DVFS algorithm monitors CPU utilization and adjusts power states accordingly. Higher utilization triggers higher voltage/frequency for performance, while lower utilization enables power savings through lower states or idle sleep.

title: Workload Prediction Algorithm
description: Predictive algorithm using moving average for proactive power management
code:
ALGORITHM Workload_Predictor
BEGIN
 history = EMPTY_QUEUE(size = N)
 
 FUNCTION predict_next_load(current_load):
 history.enqueue(current_load)
 IF history.size > N THEN
 history.dequeue()
 END IF
 
 // Simple moving average prediction
 predicted_load = sum(history) / history.size
 
 // Adjust for trend
 IF last_three_trending_up() THEN
 predicted_load = predicted_load * 1.1
 ELSE IF last_three_trending_down() THEN
 predicted_load = predicted_load * 0.9
 END IF
 
 RETURN predicted_load
 END FUNCTION
 
 // Power state decision based on prediction
 FUNCTION decide_power_state(predicted):
 IF predicted < threshold_idle THEN
 RETURN C6_SLEEP
 ELSE IF predicted < threshold_low THEN
 RETURN P4_STATE
 ELSE IF predicted < threshold_medium THEN
 RETURN P2_STATE
 ELSE
 RETURN P0_STATE
 END IF
 END FUNCTION
END
explanation: This algorithm uses historical workload data to predict future demand, enabling proactive power state transitions. This reduces the latency impact of state changes by anticipating workload shifts.

title: Thermal-Aware Task Scheduling
description: Distribute tasks across cores considering thermal constraints
code:
ALGORITHM Thermal_Aware_Scheduler
BEGIN
 WHILE new_task_arrives:
 candidates = ALL_CORES
 
 // Filter out cores above thermal threshold
 FOR each core IN candidates:
 IF core.temperature > THERMAL_LIMIT THEN
 candidates.remove(core)
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Select coolest available core
 selected_core = candidates[0]
 FOR each core IN candidates:
 IF core.temperature < selected_core.temperature THEN
 selected_core = core
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Optionally migrate running tasks if hotspot detected
 FOR each core IN ALL_CORES:
 IF core.temperature > HOTSPOT_THRESHOLD THEN
 migratable = find_low_priority_task(core)
 IF migratable AND cool_core_available() THEN
 migrate_task(migratable, cool_core)
 END IF
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 ASSIGN new_task TO selected_core
 END WHILE
END
explanation: This scheduler prevents thermal throttling by distributing tasks to cooler cores and potentially migrating existing tasks away from hotspots. This optimizes both performance and cooling energy.

complexity.time: O(C) where C = number of cores
